description:
Originally framed as VR.3.5 (typed slots in closure heap payloads). On entering the work, two pieces emerged as co-dependent:

1. **Lift the closure-reachability guard in ir_typer.** fz-ul4.27.10's narrowing skips any fn that appears in a `Prim::MakeClosure` — soundly, because closure call sites are opaque to the analysis. Without lifting this guard, closure-callable fns keep `any` entry params and schema build keeps their slots FieldKind::FzValue. There is nothing to type at the closure payload until call-site narrowing reaches through closures.

2. **Runtime closure-invoke surgery.** `fz_closure_invoke` / `fz_tail_closure` currently `copy_nonoverlapping` the captured slots blindly (8 bytes each) into the callee frame's slots [24..24+cap*8]. If the callee's entry slots are typed (RawI64/RawF64) and the closure payload still stores tagged FzValue, the copy would land tagged bits in a raw slot — silent GC corruption. The runtime needs to read each captured slot per its kind and convert into the callee slot's kind.

## Approach (pick one when this starts)

A. **Closure-callsite narrowing in ir_typer.** Track which fns each `Prim::MakeClosure` targets and which `Term::CallClosure` sites can reach them (via a flow analysis on closure values). When a callee's entire reachable-via-closure caller set is bounded, narrow entry params. Then closures aren't a special case — they get the same narrowing as direct calls. Lifts the guard.

B. **Closure-payload kind plumbing in runtime.** Add per-slot kind metadata to closure heap objects (alongside captured_count). Generate kind-tagged push helpers in fz_closure_push (or one variadic typed-push that takes a kind byte). Make fz_closure_invoke / fz_tail_closure consult the closure's slot kinds AND the callee's slot kinds (from the callee's Schema in the registry) to convert at copy time.

C. **Merge with VR.4.** VR.4 (per-fn native ABI for direct calls) could subsume this: a closure becomes a fn ptr + captures, all passed through the fn's typed ABI. No special closure-invoke runtime path. Largest scope but eliminates the special-case.

## Why deferred from .5.5

Without (1), all closure-reachable fns ship with FzValue entry params — the runtime's blind 8-byte copy is correct, just leaves the win on the table. Doing only (2) would defensively make the runtime kind-aware but with no behavior change.

User authorized the defer to keep the arc moving onto VR.4 / VR.5b / VR.6 (fz-ul4.27.5 parent is now ready to close).

## Acceptance

- A fixture exercising a closure capturing one int + one float, invoked via `f.(args)`, lowers with both captures stored raw (RawI64 + RawF64 in the closure payload).
- GC tracer parity verified (closure's per-slot kind tags drive trace decisions).
- Matrix green.

## Blocks

- Nothing in the .27.5.* chain — VR.3 (fz-ul4.27.5) closes with this filed as the remaining piece.

comments.text: Split into fz-ul4.29.5 (part A: closure-callsite flow analysis, in ir_typer) and fz-ul4.29.6 (part B: runtime closure-invoke kind-aware copy). The work in this parent ticket is fully tracked by those two.

title: VR.3.5 follow-up: typed slots in closure heap payloads
